content_en:
“There is no one here except you and me; now you can tell me freely what it is you wish to say.”

was in total darkness. He said:

“Now tell me what you wish to say. There is no need to feel shy or embarrassed. You cannot even see my face now, so why are you feeling nervous?”

Finally, with great difficulty, the scholar said,

“We are starving since many days and nights. I was too ashamed to ask you for assistance and to talk about our helplessness to you.”

“Please meet the officer of the treasury and your work will be done.”

When morning came the scholar went to the treasury and met the officer. He told him that he had received orders from the governor to give him something and asked him if he could bring some man to carry his things for him. The scholar answered that he did not have any such man and went home to his wife and scolded her, saying that the governor must have instructed his officer to provide him with some foodstuff. If he was planning to give him some money why would he need a man to carry it?

His wife told him:

“Don’t worry whatever we get will be by the will of Allah and we should submit to His Decision. Insha’ Allah, He will Provide for us. Since we are hungry even a bag of flour should be acceptable to us.”

But the scholar refused to go to the treasury a second time.

Some time later the official from the treasury came to his house and told him:

“T told the Governor that you did not have anybody to help you. So he told me to take three slaves and place three bags containing ten thousand dirhams each on their heads and reach these to your house personally. So please accept this gift from the governor. These three slaves are also gifted to you, and they will serve you.”

When the scholar saw this treatment meted out by the governor, Sa‘eed bin Al-’Aas 3, to a scholar of religion, he was overwhelmed.

“I am weeping because | am wondering how the earth will feel receiving such a generous and good man when he dies; how will the earth feel like feeding on you. You seem to me to be the incarnation of a great angel.”

Sufyan bin ‘Ainiyah narrates that when any person approached Sa’eed bin Al-‘Aas . for money, and he did not have any money on him, he would write a note and give it to the man. He would tell him to keep the note and bring it to him when he had money and claim it from him. Thus he became indebted for a very large sum. Asa result his son, ‘Amr bin Sa’eed bin Al-’ Aas, had to sell his palace in order to pay off his debts. When he was on his death-bed he sent for his son, Amr 4%, and told him to go to Amir Mu‘awiyah after his burial and give him the news of his death. If Amir Mu‘awiyah 4 offered to pay off his debts, he should not accept the offer. If he, ‘Amr 4, did not have enough money he should sell his palace in order to pay off the debts,

When Sa’eed bin Al-’Aas .# passed away he was buried in JannatulBaqi’. His son in accordance with his last will, announced:

“If my father owes any money to anyone, please come and collect it from me.”

After the burial, the obedient son set out for Syria; upon reaching there, he informed Amir Mu’awiyah 4 about the passing away of his father.

Amir Mu’awiyah 4 was grieved to hear this news and asked if Sa’eed bin Al-Aas # had any debts. ‘Amr -% answered that his father did owe money to people. Amir Mu’awiyah asked him, how much? ‘Amr - replied that his debts were to the tune of three hundred thousand dirhams.

Amir Mu’awiyah - wanted to lake over the responsibility of the debts. ‘Amr # refused saying that his father’s last wish was that if he made such an offer it should not be accepted. However, he said, if he wished to buy the palace for the amount of three hundred thousand dirhams then he would be able to sell it to him and pay off his father’s debts.

Amir Mu’awiyah bought the palace for the said amount and sent it to Al-Madinah. The son then distributed the money to the debtors.

Among the debtors was a destitute and wretched person. He was carrying slap for twenty thousand dirhams. The son was really amazed to seeing, how his father owed this beggar such a huge amount. Then the beggar told him, One day I started walking along with your father on the street. He asked me:

“Why are you walking with me?” T answered:

“I am a poor and destitute man. Having heard stories of your philanthropy and generosity { have come to ask for your help, so that I can be free of my needs. | depend on your generous nature to relieve my distress and do not think that you will disappoint me.”

Hearing this he said: “At present I do not have anything with me.”

Then he wrote a promissory note for twenty thousand dirhams and handed it to me, saying that you could collect the amount a few days later from him. This is how I have come to claim the amount of twenty thousand dirhams from you,

After hearing the poor beggar’s story the obedient son handed him the promised amount.

It was a great era in human history when people were so generous and their sons and heirs cared more deeply for a dead father’s word of honour than for their share of inheritance. The least consideration was given to things worldly. Even angels could feel proud of the noble actions and integrity of character of these great Companions of the Prophet 38.

“Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

A great Companion and Mujahid who took part in the Battle of Badr. When his sword was broken while fighting against dishelievers, the Prophet % gave him the branch of a date palm. He continued his attack on the enemy with this branch and in moments it became a sword. (Historians)

‘Ukashah bin Mohsin 2

He is one of those seventy thousand fortunate people who will enter Paradise without any reckoning or process of accountability. He was a great Companion and Mujahid who took part in the Battle of Badr. When his sword was broken while fighting against disbelievers, the Prophet 3% gave him the branch of a date palm. He continued his attack on the enemy with this branch and in moments it became a sword. This miraculous sword remained with him till the end of his life and he fought many a battle with it and shattered to pieces many enemies.

His sister, Umm Qais bint Mohsin narrated:

“My brother was very good-looking. He excelled by virtue of nobility of birth and was superior to others by virtue of knowledge and learning. He was also a great leader and ruler. When the Prophet # passed away he was twenty-four years old. He had taken part in the Battle of Badr and the Battle of Uhud and other battles with great favor and enthusiasm. He displayed exceptional expertise in many wars.

The Prophet 3% declared that seventy thousand individuals from his Ummah would enter Paradise without any accounting. ‘Ukashah was seated near him. He immediately asked if his name was also among them. The Prophet 3% answered that his name was among them. Another person, who was seated beside him, asked the same question. But the Prophet 38 said ‘Ukashah had taken precedence over him.

Paradise without being answerable for one’s deeds is the very peak of suspiciousness and good fortune. ‘Ukashah bin Mohsin was indeed a very fortunate man!

He was the son of Mohsin bin Harsaén and was a scion of the Banu Asad tribe. Even before the migration of the Prophet 3% he had sworn allegiance to him in person. Then he had the honour of migrating to Al-Madinah.

He fought bravely and heroically in the Battle of Badr and the Battle of Uhud and many other battles. This personification of beauty, both inward and outward spent his whole life for Jihaéd and left unforgettable tales of heroism in the annals of military history. He is among some of the greatest Companions of the Prophet 3%.

On the way from Al-Madinah to Najad is Ghamar, which was under the control of the Banu Asad. It is a small place with a fresh water spring, hence has great value in the desert. Travelers often stopped here to take rest and drink the pure waters of this stream.

The Prophet 38 appointed ‘Ukashah -# as the Commander of a small but loyal band of forty Mujahideen to attack Ghamar. ‘Ukashah bin Mohsin led his small army of men very professionally, not allowing the enemy to stabilize themselves. They were taken by surprise at the sudden and swift advance of the Mujahideen. Instead of facing them the Banu Asad fled leaving the spring and their livestock behind. The Mujahideen got two hundred camels and some goats; and they took these to Al-Madinah. The Prophet 3£ was very happy that ‘Ukashah had shown such military and leadership skills in the very first enterprise entrusted to him.
content_id:
"Tidak ada siapa pun di sini kecuali engkau dan aku; sekarang engkau bisa menceritakan dengan bebas apa yang ingin engkau sampaikan."

dalam kegelapan total. Beliau berkata:

"Sekarang katakanlah apa yang ingin engkau sampaikan. Tidak perlu merasa malu atau sungkan. Engkau bahkan tidak bisa melihat wajahku sekarang, jadi mengapa engkau merasa gugup?"

Akhirnya, dengan sangat susah payah, ulama tersebut berkata,

"Kami telah kelaparan selama berhari-hari dan bermalam-malam. Aku terlalu malu untuk meminta bantuan kepadamu dan menceritakan keadaan kami yang tidak berdaya ini."

"Silakan temui petugas perbendaharaan dan urusanmu akan diselesaikan."

Ketika pagi tiba, ulama tersebut pergi ke perbendaharaan dan menemui petugas di sana. Petugas itu memberitahunya bahwa ia telah menerima perintah dari gubernur untuk memberikan sesuatu kepadanya dan bertanya apakah ia bisa membawa seseorang untuk mengangkut barang-barangnya. Ulama tersebut menjawab bahwa ia tidak memiliki orang seperti itu, lalu pulang ke rumah dan memarahi istrinya, mengatakan bahwa gubernur pasti telah memerintahkan petugasnya untuk memberikan bahan makanan. Jika ia berencana memberikan uang, mengapa ia membutuhkan orang untuk mengangkutnya?

Istrinya berkata kepadanya:

"Jangan khawatir, apa pun yang kita terima adalah atas kehendak Allah dan kita harus berserah diri kepada Keputusan-Nya. Insya Allah, Dia akan Memberi rezeki kepada kita. Karena kita sedang kelaparan, bahkan sekarung tepung pun seharusnya bisa kita terima dengan syukur."

Namun ulama tersebut menolak pergi ke perbendaharaan untuk kedua kalinya.

Beberapa waktu kemudian petugas perbendaharaan datang ke rumahnya dan berkata kepadanya:

"Aku memberitahu Gubernur bahwa engkau tidak memiliki siapa pun untuk membantumu. Maka beliau memerintahkanku untuk membawa tiga budak dan menaruh tiga kantong yang masing-masing berisi sepuluh ribu dirham di atas kepala mereka dan mengantarkannya ke rumahmu secara pribadi. Jadi terimalah hadiah ini dari gubernur. Tiga budak ini juga dihadiahkan untukmu, dan mereka akan melayanimu."

Ketika ulama tersebut melihat perlakuan yang diberikan oleh gubernur, Sa'eed bin Al-'Aas radhiyallahu 'anhu, kepada seorang ulama agama, ia sangat terharu.

"Aku menangis karena aku bertanya-tanya bagaimana perasaan bumi ketika menerima orang yang begitu dermawan dan baik saat ia wafat; bagaimana perasaan bumi memakan jasadmu. Engkau tampak bagiku bagaikan perwujudan malaikat yang agung."

Sufyan bin 'Ainiyah meriwayatkan bahwa ketika seseorang mendatangi Sa'eed bin Al-'Aas radhiyallahu 'anhu untuk meminta uang, dan ia tidak memiliki uang, ia akan menulis sebuah catatan dan memberikannya kepada orang tersebut. Ia akan menyuruhnya menyimpan catatan itu dan membawanya kembali kepadanya ketika ia memiliki uang untuk mengklaimnya. Dengan demikian ia menjadi berhutang dalam jumlah yang sangat besar. Akibatnya putranya, 'Amr bin Sa'eed bin Al-'Aas, harus menjual istananya untuk melunasi hutang-hutangnya. Ketika ia berada di ranjang kematiannya, ia memanggil putranya, 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu, dan menyuruhnya pergi menemui Amir Mu'awiyah setelah pemakamannya dan memberitahu kabar kematiannya. Jika Amir Mu'awiyah radhiyallahu 'anhu menawarkan untuk melunasi hutang-hutangnya, ia tidak boleh menerima tawaran tersebut. Jika ia, 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu, tidak memiliki cukup uang, ia harus menjual istananya untuk melunasi hutang-hutang itu.

Ketika Sa'eed bin Al-'Aas radhiyallahu 'anhu wafat, beliau dimakamkan di Jannatul Baqi'. Putranya, sesuai wasiat terakhirnya, mengumumkan:

"Jika ayahku berhutang uang kepada siapa pun, silakan datang dan ambil dari saya."

Setelah pemakaman, putra yang taat itu berangkat menuju Syam; setibanya di sana, ia memberitahu Amir Mu'awiyah radhiyallahu 'anhu tentang wafatnya ayahnya.

Amir Mu'awiyah radhiyallahu 'anhu sangat berduka mendengar kabar ini dan bertanya apakah Sa'eed bin Al-'Aas radhiyallahu 'anhu memiliki hutang. 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu menjawab bahwa ayahnya memang berhutang kepada orang-orang. Amir Mu'awiyah bertanya berapa jumlahnya. 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu menjawab bahwa hutangnya mencapai tiga ratus ribu dirham.

Amir Mu'awiyah radhiyallahu 'anhu ingin mengambil alih tanggung jawab atas hutang-hutang tersebut. 'Amr radhiyallahu 'anhu menolak dengan mengatakan bahwa wasiat terakhir ayahnya adalah jika ia membuat tawaran seperti itu maka tidak boleh diterima. Namun, katanya, jika ia berkenan membeli istana tersebut seharga tiga ratus ribu dirham maka ia akan bisa menjualnya kepadanya dan melunasi hutang-hutang ayahnya.

Amir Mu'awiyah membeli istana tersebut dengan harga yang disebutkan dan mengirimkannya ke Al-Madinah. Sang putra kemudian membagikan uang tersebut kepada para pemiutang.

Di antara para pemiutang terdapat seorang yang miskin dan sengsara. Ia membawa catatan hutang sebesar dua puluh ribu dirham. Sang putra benar-benar takjub melihat bagaimana ayahnya berhutang sebesar itu kepada pengemis ini. Kemudian pengemis itu bercerita kepadanya: Suatu hari aku berjalan bersama ayahmu di jalanan. Beliau bertanya kepadaku:

"Mengapa engkau berjalan bersamaku?" Aku menjawab:

"Aku adalah orang miskin dan tidak berpunya. Setelah mendengar kisah-kisah tentang kedermawanan dan kemurahan hatimu, aku datang untuk meminta bantuanmu, agar aku bisa terbebas dari kebutuhanku. Aku bergantung pada sifat dermawanmu untuk meringankan penderitaanku dan tidak berpikir bahwa engkau akan mengecewakanku."

Mendengar ini beliau berkata: "Saat ini aku tidak memiliki apa-apa."

Kemudian beliau menulis surat janji hutang sebesar dua puluh ribu dirham dan menyerahkannya kepadaku, sambil berkata bahwa aku bisa mengambil uangnya beberapa hari kemudian darinya. Beginilah aku datang untuk mengklaim jumlah dua puluh ribu dirham darimu.

Setelah mendengar cerita pengemis miskin itu, sang putra yang taat menyerahkan jumlah yang dijanjikan kepadanya.

Itu adalah era yang agung dalam sejarah manusia ketika orang-orang begitu dermawan dan anak-anak serta ahli waris mereka lebih mementingkan kehormatan kata-kata ayah mereka yang telah wafat daripada bagian warisan mereka. Hal-hal duniawi diberikan pertimbangan paling sedikit. Bahkan para malaikat pun bisa merasa bangga dengan perbuatan mulia dan integritas karakter para Sahabat Nabi ﷺ yang agung ini.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang tenang lagi diridhai-Nya!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

Seorang Sahabat agung dan Mujahid yang ikut serta dalam Perang Badr. Ketika pedangnya patah saat bertempur melawan orang-orang kafir, Nabi ﷺ memberikannya dahan pohon kurma. Ia melanjutkan serangannya terhadap musuh dengan dahan ini dan dalam sekejap dahan itu berubah menjadi pedang. (Para Sejarawan)

'Ukashah bin Mohsin radhiyallahu 'anhu

Beliau adalah salah satu dari tujuh puluh ribu orang beruntung yang akan memasuki Surga tanpa hisab atau proses pertanggungjawaban apa pun. Beliau adalah seorang Sahabat agung dan Mujahid yang ikut serta dalam Perang Badr. Ketika pedangnya patah saat bertempur melawan orang-orang kafir, Nabi ﷺ memberikannya dahan pohon kurma. Beliau melanjutkan serangannya terhadap musuh dengan dahan ini dan dalam sekejap dahan itu berubah menjadi pedang. Pedang ajaib ini tetap bersamanya hingga akhir hayatnya dan beliau bertempur dalam banyak pertempuran dengannya serta menghancurkan banyak musuh.

Saudarinya, Umm Qais bint Mohsin meriwayatkan:

"Saudaraku sangat tampan. Ia unggul berkat kemuliaan nasabnya dan lebih utama dari yang lain berkat ilmu dan pengetahuannya. Ia juga seorang pemimpin dan penguasa yang hebat. Ketika Nabi ﷺ wafat, ia berusia dua puluh empat tahun. Ia telah ikut serta dalam Perang Badr dan Perang Uhud serta pertempuran-pertempuran lainnya dengan penuh semangat dan antusiasme. Ia menunjukkan keahlian luar biasa dalam banyak peperangan.

Nabi ﷺ menyatakan bahwa tujuh puluh ribu individu dari umat beliau akan memasuki Surga tanpa hisab apa pun. 'Ukashah sedang duduk di dekat beliau. Ia segera bertanya apakah namanya juga termasuk di antara mereka. Nabi ﷺ menjawab bahwa namanya termasuk di antara mereka. Orang lain, yang duduk di sampingnya, mengajukan pertanyaan yang sama. Namun Nabi ﷺ bersabda bahwa 'Ukashah telah mendahuluinya.

Masuk Surga tanpa dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas amal perbuatan seseorang adalah puncak tertinggi dari keberuntungan dan kebahagiaan. 'Ukashah bin Mohsin memang seorang yang sangat beruntung!

Beliau adalah putra Mohsin bin Harsaén dan merupakan keturunan suku Banu Asad. Bahkan sebelum hijrah Nabi ﷺ, beliau telah berbaiat kepadanya secara langsung. Kemudian beliau mendapat kehormatan untuk berhijrah ke Al-Madinah.

Beliau bertempur dengan gagah berani dan heroik dalam Perang Badr dan Perang Uhud serta banyak pertempuran lainnya. Perwujudan keindahan lahir dan batin ini menghabiskan seluruh hidupnya untuk jihad dan meninggalkan kisah-kisah kepahlawanan yang tak terlupakan dalam catatan sejarah militer. Beliau termasuk di antara para Sahabat terbesar Nabi ﷺ.

Di jalan dari Al-Madinah menuju Najd terdapat Ghamar, yang berada di bawah kendali Banu Asad. Tempat ini kecil dengan mata air segar, sehingga sangat berharga di padang pasir. Para musafir sering singgah di sini untuk beristirahat dan meminum air murni dari mata air ini.

Nabi ﷺ menunjuk 'Ukashah radhiyallahu 'anhu sebagai Komandan sekelompok kecil namun setia yang terdiri dari empat puluh Mujahidin untuk menyerang Ghamar. 'Ukashah bin Mohsin memimpin pasukannya yang kecil dengan sangat profesional, tidak memberi kesempatan musuh untuk menstabilkan diri. Mereka terkejut oleh serangan mendadak dan cepat para Mujahidin. Alih-alih menghadapi mereka, Banu Asad melarikan diri meninggalkan mata air dan ternak mereka. Para Mujahidin mendapatkan dua ratus unta dan beberapa kambing; dan mereka membawanya ke Al-Madinah. Nabi ﷺ sangat senang bahwa 'Ukashah telah menunjukkan kemampuan militer dan kepemimpinan yang luar biasa dalam tugas pertama yang dipercayakan kepadanya.
